---
title: "01_Preprocessing"
author: "Zulema Carracedo Lorenzo"
date: "2023-10-05"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
#summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
#plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

#Load packages
```{r}
library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(vegan)
library(decontam)
```

# Load data

##OTU table
```{r}
# Load OTU table
otu <- otu_table(object = read.table(file = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/data/novaseq_ernakovich_output/seqtab_final.txt", 
                                              header = TRUE,
                                              row.names = 1, # first column has row names (ASV names)
                                              check.names = FALSE), # prevents "X" to be added to column names
                                              taxa_are_rows = TRUE)


```

## Taxonomy tabe

```{r}
# Load taxonomy able
taxtab <-read.table(file = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/data/novaseq_ernakovich_output/taxonomy.tsv", 
                               header = TRUE,
                               sep = "\t",
                               row.names = 1, # first column has row names (ASV names)
                               check.names = FALSE) # prevents "X" to be added to column names, such as X49_16S

# adjusts number and name of columns
taxtab<- separate(data = taxtab,
                          col = Taxon,
                          into =c("Kingdom", 
                                       "Phylum", 
                                       "Class", 
                                       "Order", 
                                       "Family",
                                       "Genus", 
                                       "Species"),
                               sep = ";")
head(taxtab) #check

# saves taxtable as phyloseq object
taxtab<-tax_table(object = as.matrix(taxtab))
```


##Sequences
```{r}
# Load sequences 
refseq <- refseq(physeq = Biostrings::readDNAStringSet(filepath = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/data/novaseq_ernakovich_output/repset.fasta",
                                                       use.names = TRUE)) 

head(taxa_names(refseq)) #check


```

## Metadata
```{r}
#Load metadata

metadata <- read.table(file = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/data/novaseq_ernakovich_output/sample_data.txt", 
                                                  header = TRUE,
                                                  sep = "\t",
                                                  row.names = 1, # first column has row names (ASV names)
                                                  check.names = FALSE)

#check structure of dataframe

str(metadata)
colnames(metadata) #correct
rownames(metadata) #sample-ID

#Finally make it a sample_data object for phyloseq 
metadata<- sample_data(metadata)
```

##Make a phyloseq object
```{r}
#Merge to create phyloseq 
ps <- merge_phyloseq(metadata, otu, taxtab, refseq) #65,144 taxa, 95 samples

#Add total number of reads
ps@sam_data$Library_size <- sample_sums(ps)

#Kep the phyloseq object and remove the single objects
rm(metadata, otu, taxtab, refseq)

#save ps object 
save(ps, file = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/r_objects/ps.RData")
load(file = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/r_objects/ps.RData")

# run garbage collection after creating large objects
gc()

```

## Let's have a first look to the data

```{r}
#quick check on the number of reads 
sums <- sample_sums(ps)
sums[order(sums)]

# Lowest and highest total count:
min(sums) #259,522
max(sums) #770,824

# Sample with lowest counts:
names(sums[order(sums)][1]) #it's a sample of root associated (ZSB-51)

#see the correlation between number of reads and diversity of ASVs
t_otu <- t(otu_table(ps)) #transpose observations to rows, needed for vegan package
class(t_otu) <- "matrix" #it needs to be forced to be a matrix, if not vegan gives me an error for the rarecurve function
rarecurve((t_otu), step=50, cex=0.5) 

ggsave("rarecurve.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.3)

```


##Decontaminate 
# More info https://benjjneb.github.io/decontam/vignettes/decontam_intro.html

```{r}

#Let's have a look to the library sizes
df <- as.data.frame(sample_data(ps)) # Put sample_data into a ggplot-friendly data.frame
#df$LibrarySize <- sample_sums(ps)
df <- df[order(df$Library_size),]
df$Index <- seq(nrow(df))
ggplot(data=df, aes(x=Index, y=Library_size, color=Sample_type)) + geom_point()
ggplot(data=df, aes(x=Index, y=Library_size, color=water_regime)) + geom_point()

# I will use the function isContaminant with the frequency method.  In this method, the distribution of the frequency of each sequence feature as a function of the input DNA concentration is used to identify contaminants.

#Because the blanks of this experiment were not amplified during the library preparation, we can't use the prevalence method

contamdf<- isContaminant(ps,
                               method="frequency",
                               conc="amplicon_concentration_ngul",
                               
                               batch=NULL, #all samples correspond to the same experiments, change when new sequenced data comes
                               threshold=0.05) #default threshold is 0.1

head(contamdf)
table(contamdf$contaminant) #319 ASVs contaminants, that's  0.49%
which(contamdf$contaminant) # 319 out of 65144 ASVs were classified as contaminants, some include very abundant sequences including the 14th and 15th most abundant



# Check which taxa are contaminants

ps_contam <- prune_taxa(contamdf$contaminant, ps)
tax_table(ps_contam)
View(as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_contam)))
plot_bar(ps_contam, "Phylum", fill="Class") #phyllum actinobacteriota and proteobacteria are the most contaminants
ggsave("contamASVs.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.3)


#Compare a good ASV with a contaminant ASV
plot_frequency(ps, taxa_names(ps)[c(2,15)], conc="amplicon_concentration_ngul") + 
  xlab("DNA Concentration ")

#visualized all contaminant
contaminants_v <- which(contamdf$contaminant)
plot_frequency(ps, taxa_names(ps)[contaminants_v], conc="amplicon_concentration_ngul") + 
  xlab("DNA Concentration ")

ggsave("contam_freq.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.3)

set.seed(100)
plot_frequency(ps, taxa_names(ps)[sample(which(contamdf$contaminant),20)],
               conc="amplicon_concentration_ngul")+
               xlab("DNA concentration (ngul)")+
    ggtitle("Contaminant ASVs")

ggsave("contam_freq_20.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.3)

#Which samples have the highest abundance of contaminants?
p_cont <- plot_bar(ps_contam, fill="Phylum")

p_cont + facet_wrap(~Sample_type, nrow=1, scales="free_x") +
  ggtitle("samples with the highest abundance of contaminants (319 ASVs)")

# soil samples contain more contaminants than rhizosphere samples

ggsave("contamASVs_per_sample.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.3)


#Make a new phyloseq object by removing the contaminant ASVs
contam_ASVs <- subset(contamdf, contaminant=="TRUE") 
contam_ASVs <- row.names(contam_ASVs)
ps_decontam <- prune_taxa(!taxa_names(ps) %in% contam_ASVs, ps) #319 contam ASVs have been removed
#remove those ASVs with now 0 reads 
prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_decontam) >0, ps_decontam)

#What proportion of our count data were removed as contaminants?
pre <- sum(sample_sums(ps))
post <- sum(sample_sums(ps_decontam))

(pre-post) / pre #only 0.06%

#plot final ps to check if the condition is not matched
set.seed(100)
plot_frequency(seqtab = ps_decontam, 
               taxa = sample(taxa_names(ps_decontam),20),
               conc="amplicon_concentration_ngul")+
    #aes(color = libsize_after_filtering)+
    #scale_color_viridis()+
    ggtitle("Real ASVs") #doesn't look good, check again

ggsave("Real_ASVs_freq_20.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.3)

#check number of reads 
plot(sort(sample_sums(ps_decontam))) #still some samples with very few reads 

#make new column with this information
ps_decontam@sam_data$libsize_after_decontam <- sample_sums(ps_decontam)

sort(ps_decontam@sam_data$libsize_after_decontam) #some samples still have low number of reads
```


##Filtering

##Non-bacterial ASVs and plastid dna

```{r}
#Remove non-bacterial ASVs

ps_Bac <- subset_taxa(ps_decontam, Kingdom=="d__Bacteria") #down to 64761 taxa

#Chloroplast contamination
p_Chloro <- plot_bar(subset_taxa(ps_Bac, Order==" o__Chloroplast"), fill="Species")
p_Chloro + facet_wrap(~Sample_type, nrow=1, scales="free_x") +
  ggtitle("Chloroplast contamination") 

#sample ZSB-51 has much higher chloroplast contamination than other samples. This is very surprisin as it is a root associated samples

ggsave("Bac.Chloroplast-contam.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.5)

#mitochondria
p_Mito <- plot_bar(subset_taxa(ps_Bac, Family==" f__Mitochondria"), fill="Species")
p_Mito + facet_wrap(~Sample_type, nrow=1, scales="free_x") +
  ggtitle("Mitochondria contamination")

ggsave("Bac.Mitochondria-contam.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.5)

#clean 
rm(p_Chloro, p_Mito)

#remove plastid reads
Mitochondria_ps<- subset_taxa(ps_Bac, Family==" f__Mitochondria" | Family == "Mitochondria") #there's 26 taxa 
Chloroplast_ps <-subset_taxa(ps_Bac, Order == " o__Chloroplast" | Order == "Chloroplast") #there's 88 taxa

host_plant_ps<-merge_phyloseq(Chloroplast_ps, Mitochondria_ps) #114 taxa

#quick histogram showing plant DNA contamination
hist(sample_sums(host_plant_ps)/sample_sums(ps_Bac)*100, breaks = 100) #from 0 to 40% host DNA contamination contamination

#investigate the most abundant chloroplast ASV
tax_table(Chloroplast_ps)
View(as.data.frame(refseq(Chloroplast_ps)))
#when I blast the sequence of ASV_3085 I get match with Camelina sativa

#ZSB-51 has a 0.4% plastid contamination. The next sample with the most plastid contamination has 0.08%

# define host plant 16S contamination as metadata
ps_Bac@sam_data$Mitochondria_reads<-sample_sums(Mitochondria_ps)
ps_Bac@sam_data$Chloroplast_reads<-sample_sums(Chloroplast_ps)
ps_Bac@sam_data$Plastid_reads<-sample_sums(host_plant_ps)
ps_Bac@sam_data$Plastid_contamination_pct<-sample_sums(host_plant_ps)/sample_sums(ps_Bac)*100

#Remove plastid ASVs
# prune_taxa only works with those to keep not to "keep out", thus a little trick is needed
# to make a new vector: https://github.com/joey711/phyloseq/issues/652

plastid_taxa <- taxa_names(host_plant_ps)
all_taxa <- taxa_names(ps_Bac)
taxa_to_keep <- all_taxa[!all_taxa %in% plastid_taxa] 

ps_Bac_plastid <- prune_taxa(taxa_to_keep, ps_Bac) #64647 taxa

#compare 
ps_Bac #old ps
ps_Bac_plastid #new ps filtered without plastid reads (-354 ASVs) 20,565 ASVs

mean(sample_sums(ps_Bac_plastid))/mean(sample_sums(ps_Bac))*100 #99% of reads kept

#clean
rm(Mitochondria_ps, Chloroplast_ps, host_plant_ps)
   
```

## Low reads and non-informative ASVs

```{r}
#check number of reads after plastid dna removal
sums <- sample_sums(ps_Bac_plastid)
sums[order(sums)]

#add number of reads after plastid
ps_Bac_plastid@sam_data$libsize_after_plastid<-sample_sums(ps_Bac_plastid)

#remove samples with low number of reads, removing those with less than 10,000 reads
#as it jumps from 59 to 16,474 and continues to increase.
ps_filtered <- subset_samples(ps_Bac_plastid, libsize_after_plastid > 10000) 

#remove ASVs with low-length sequences
#check final sequence length. 16S sequences smaller than 380bp should be discarded
summary(ps_filtered@refseq@ranges@width) 
hist(as.data.frame(refseq(ps_filtered)@ranges)$width, breaks = 50)

#remove the ones smaller than 380bp (expected amplicon size)
ps_filtered<- prune_taxa(taxa = as.data.frame(refseq(ps_filtered)@ranges)$width>380,
                       x = ps_filtered) #21 ASVs removed

#remove those ASVs with now 0 reads 
prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_filtered) >0, ps_filtered)

#let's see how many sequences were kept after this filtering
sum(sample_sums(ps_filtered))/sum(sample_sums(ps_Bac_plastid))*100 # 99.99% of sequences kept

#add column to sample_data
ps_filtered@sam_data$libsize_after_filtering <- sample_sums(ps_filtered)

#check how many reads were kept at the end
ps_filtered@sam_data$pct_reads_kept <- ((ps_filtered@sam_data$libsize_after_filtering/ps_filtered@sam_data$Library_size)*100)

summary(ps_filtered@sam_data$pct_reads_kept) #Min of 83.32% of the reads were kept

```

## Check filtering

```{r}
#make it a df to plot it 
df_filtered <-data.frame(sample_data(ps_filtered))
df_filtered <- tibble::rownames_to_column(df_filtered, "Sample_name") #row names into column
order_samp_name <- df_filtered$Sample_name

#make Harvest ID a factor to have a better plot
df_filtered$Pot_number <- factor(df_filtered$Sample_name, levels=order_samp_name)

#see it 
ggplot(df_filtered, aes(x = pct_reads_kept, y = Sample_name, label = Sample_name)) +    
       geom_point(aes(colour = Sample_type, shape = water_regime)) +
       geom_text(aes(label = Sample_name), hjust = - 0.5)
       #facet_wrap(~Experiment, scales="free_x")

ggsave("Filtered_pctg_conta.png", path = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/results/plots/exploration",
         width = 40, height = 20 , units = "in", dpi=300, scale=0.3)


```

##Final ps
```{r}
#last check of filtering steps

ps # 65,144 raw phyloseq object
ps_decontam # 64,825 after decontam package
ps_Bac # 64,761 after removing non-bacterial sequences
ps_Bac_plastid # 64,674 after removing chloroplast and mitochondria contamination
ps_filtered # 64,626 after removing non-informative sequences


#save ps object 
save(ps_filtered, file = "./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/r_objects/ps_filtered.RData")
load("./5_Brassica_oleracea_exp/r_objects/ps_filtered.RData")

#clean
rm(ps_16S_raw, ps_decontam, ps_plastid, ps_16S_filtering, plot_Bac)
```
